title: Post-stain protocol for GreenGlo™ Safe DNA Dye, 20,000X in Water
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b77ec","text":"GreenGlo ™, 20,000X in Water, is a non-carcinogenic and non-toxic alternative to Ethidium bromide used for the detection of nucleic acids in agarose gels. It is as sensitive as Ethidium bromide. There is no toxic DMSO as GreenGlo ™ is supplied in water. \n \nGreenGlo ™ has fluorescence excitation maxima at 295 nm and 490 nm. The fluorescence emission maxima is similar to EtBr when bound to DNA – at 530 nm.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"mfLzR","text":"MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":null},{"key":"fygI6","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":3688,"guid":"4105F46C341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"GreenGlo™ Safe DNA Dye, 20,000X in Water, 500µl","mol_weight":0,"sku":"CA3600","url":"https://www.denvillescientific.com/products/greenglo%E2%84%A2-safe-dna-dye--20-000x-in-water--500%C2%B5l","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":129,"name":"Denville Scientific Inc.","link":"https://www.denvillescientific.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Use 10 to 15 µl per 100 ml of staining solution. Same as when using ethidium bromide. If pre-staining follow: Pre-stain protocol for GreenGlo™ Safe DNA Dye .
For an average gel thickness of about 7 mm, stain 30 minutes. 00:03:00
Destain of 30 minutes in water. 00:30:00 Protect gel and staining solution from light with aluminum foil or place in dark.
Detect bands under UV illumination (yellow or green gelatin- or cellophane filters is recommended for clearer bands) or non-UV LED illuminators such as Blue Light LED illumination.
creator: Nicole Clouse
